content:
I am a graduate student at the Department of Astronomy and Steward Observatory at The University of Arizona in Tucson (AZ). I work with Vasilis Paschalidis on various topics in gravitational physics. In particular, I use and extend the Einstein Toolkit to perform dynamical-spacetime simulations of rotating relativistic stars, and of systems with charged black holes. Moreover, I live inside GNU Emacs.
If you want to know more about me, you can read my academic curriculum vitae or you can send me an email.

title: Gabriele Bozzola

summary: We find that the supramassive limit for hybrid stars is non-universal, but other universalities hold.
title: Maximum mass and universal relations of rotating relativistic hybrid hadron-quark stars

summary: We apply the Bowen-York formalism to generate initial data for charged black-hole systems.
title: Initial data for general relativistic simulations of multiple electrically charged black holes with linear and angular momenta

summary: Turning points of sequences of rotating stars satisfy relations independent on the EOS and the degree of differential rotation.
title: Universal relations for differentially rotating relativistic stars at the threshold to collapse
